moral.title: Truth
moral.summary: Speak what is real, even when it is costly. A society that tolerates routine deception cannot govern itself.

moral.title: Justice
moral.summary: Render to each what is owed. Treat equals equally and unequals proportionally. Refuse the temptation to bend a rule for a favored party.

moral.title: Courage
moral.summary: Do the right thing under pressure. Courage is not the absence of fear; it is action ordered by principle when fear is present.

moral.title: Prudence
moral.summary: Choose well among real options. Wisdom in action — neither reckless nor paralyzed, but proportionate to circumstance.

moral.title: Temperance
moral.summary: Govern your appetites before they govern you. Self-mastery is the foundation of every other freedom.

moral.title: Charity
moral.summary: Will the good of the other. Not sentimentality, but a steady commitment to the dignity of every person you encounter.

moral.title: Faithfulness
moral.summary: Keep your word. Promises kept are the unseen architecture of every functioning community.

moral.title: Humility
moral.summary: Know what you do not know. Most catastrophes in history were caused by people certain they were right.

logic.title: Identity
logic.summary: A thing is what it is. Words must mean something stable, or argument is impossible.

logic.title: Non-Contradiction
logic.summary: A claim and its opposite cannot both be true in the same sense at the same time.

logic.title: Sufficient Reason
logic.summary: Every claim should rest on evidence or argument proportionate to its weight.

logic.title: Distinguish Premises from Conclusions
logic.summary: Most disagreements are not about conclusions — they are about hidden assumptions. Surface them.

logic.title: Beware of Common Fallacies
logic.summary: Ad hominem, straw man, appeal to authority, false dilemma, slippery slope. Learn to name them so you can answer them.

logic.title: Steelman Before You Critique
logic.summary: State the strongest version of a view before you disagree with it. Anything less is sport, not thought.
